commits.messageHeadline: refactor: extract chat webview assets to separate file

latestReviews.body:
## Code Review

This pull request refactors the chat webview's CSS and JavaScript assets out of minified string literals in `chatView.ts` and into a new, more maintainable `src/webview/chatAssets.ts` file. This is a positive change for code organization and readability. My review identified two minor regressions in the new CSS file: an accessibility issue with the copy button's focus visibility and a visual bug with the typing indicator animation. The rest of the changes are primarily code formatting, which improves readability. I've also suggested adding unit tests to prevent accessibility regressions.

### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `a98463b68e`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

**Actionable comments posted: 4**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@src/chatView.ts`:
- Around line 318-336: The handler registered in
webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage uses several single-line if statements
without braces (e.g., the check for message?.type === "requestInitialState",
message?.type !== "sendMessage", and the empty sessionId/text check), which
triggers static-analysis warnings; update those conditions in the
onDidReceiveMessage callback to always use block braces { ... } around their
bodies (so the call to this.postState(), the early return when type !==
"sendMessage", and the early return when !sessionId || !text are wrapped in
braces) to satisfy the linter and make the control flow explicit while leaving
the try { await this.onSendMessage(sessionId, text); } catch (...) block
unchanged.
- Around line 120-125: The linter warns about a missing block after the if
statement in the isGeneratingSessionState function; update the function so the
if (!rawState) branch uses braces (i.e., wrap the return false; in { ... }) to
satisfy the rule—modify the isGeneratingSessionState implementation that
references rawState and GENERATING_SESSION_STATES.has(rawState) accordingly.
- Around line 208-236: Add missing block braces for every single-line if in
chatView.ts so the parser stops warning; specifically wrap the bodies of if
(activity.sessionFailed?.reason), if (activity.planGenerated?.plan), if
((activity as any).gitPatch?.diff) and its inner if (typeof diff === "string" &&
diff.trim().length > 0), as well as if (activity.artifacts &&
activity.artifacts.length > 0) and the nested if (artifact.changeSet) and its
inner if (diffData && typeof diffData === "string") with { ... } blocks; update
the surrounding code that builds detailsHtml in the functions/methods handling
activity rendering to use these braced blocks so the linter/CI warnings
disappear.

In `@src/webview/chatAssets.ts`:
- Around line 39-121: Add a new unit test file specifically for this module that
imports CHAT_JS and verifies the webview script behavior: mount a DOM with
elements used by the script (chat, typing, messageInput, sendButton,
sessionLabel, composer) then execute the CHAT_JS script in a jsdom environment
and assert renderMessages/updateUI outcomes by posting a "chatState" message
(check that messages with properties matching ChatMessageItem render correctly
and timestamps are formatted), simulate input/submit and Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to
ensure vscode.postMessage is called with {type:"sendMessage"}, and test the
copy-button click flow (mock navigator.clipboard.writeText to succeed/fail and
assert button text changes). Ensure tests also cover the fallback
acquireVsCodeApi case by providing a fake acquireVsCodeApi absence and spying
the console fallback postMessage, and name the file e.g.
src/webview/chatAssets.test.ts to satisfy module-level coverage.
```

</details>

---

<summary>ℹ️ Review info</summary>

pr_view.title: Refactor chat webview assets out of minified literals

nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep copy button visible when keyboard-focused**

The stylesheet now only reveals `.copy-code-button` on hover, but the button remains `opacity: 0` during keyboard navigation. In the webview this means keyboard-only users can tab to the copy control without any visible focus indication, so the copy action is effectively undiscoverable unless a mouse hover happens. Please include a focus-visible selector in the reveal rule (as before) so accessibility and keyboard usability are preserved.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**タイピングドットのアニメーション遅延セレクターが一致しない** `typing` div の HTML 構造は以下のとおりです： ```html
Jules is working ← nth-child(1)（テキスト、.typing-dot ではない） ← nth-child(2) ← nth-child(3) ← nth-child(4)
``` `.typing-dot:nth-child(1)` は、1番目の子要素（`Jules is working`）が `.typing-dot` クラスを持たないため、**どの要素にも一致しません**。そのため、3つのドットのうち最初のドット（`nth-child(2)`）のみに `-0.16s` の遅延が適用され、残り2つには遅延が設定されません。 以前の CSS では `nth-child(2)` と `nth-child(3)` を使用しており、3つのドットのうち2つに遅延を付与していました。修正案： ```suggestion .typing-dot:nth-child(2) { animation-delay: -0.32s; } .typing-dot:nth-child(3) { animation-delay: -0.16s; } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/webview/chatAssets.ts Line: 26-27 Comment: **タイピングドットのアニメーション遅延セレクターが一致しない** `typing` div の HTML 構造は以下のとおりです： ```html
Jules is working ← nth-child(1)（テキスト、.typing-dot ではない） ← nth-child(2) ← nth-child(3) ← nth-child(4)
``` `.typing-dot:nth-child(1)` は、1番目の子要素（`Jules is working`）が `.typing-dot` クラスを持たないため、**どの要素にも一致しません**。そのため、3つのドットのうち最初のドット（`nth-child(2)`）のみに `-0.16s` の遅延が適用され、残り2つには遅延が設定されません。 以前の CSS では `nth-child(2)` と `nth-child(3)` を使用しており、3つのドットのうち2つに遅延を付与していました。修正案： ```suggestion .typing-dot:nth-child(2) { animation-delay: -0.32s; } .typing-dot:nth-child(3) { animation-delay: -0.16s; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**コピーボタンのキーボードフォーカス時の視認性が失われた（アクセシビリティ後退）** 旧 CSS では以下のようなセレクターが存在していました： ```css .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ``` 新しい CSS では `:focus-visible` の部分が削除されており、`.code-block:hover .copy-code-button` のみになっています。これにより、キーボードでタブ移動してコピーボタンにフォーカスした際にボタンが表示されなくなり、キーボード操作のアクセシビリティが低下します。 ```suggestion .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/webview/chatAssets.ts Line: 19 Comment: **コピーボタンのキーボードフォーカス時の視認性が失われた（アクセシビリティ後退）** 旧 CSS では以下のようなセレクターが存在していました： ```css .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ``` 新しい CSS では `:focus-visible` の部分が削除されており、`.code-block:hover .copy-code-button` のみになっています。これにより、キーボードでタブ移動してコピーボタンにフォーカスした際にボタンが表示されなくなり、キーボード操作のアクセシビリティが低下します。 ```suggestion .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**`:focus-visible` から `:focus` への変更によるアクセシビリティの後退** 旧 CSS では `#messageInput:focus-visible` を使用していましたが、新 CSS では `#messageInput:focus` に変わっています。`:focus` はマウスクリック時にもアウトラインを表示するため、視覚的にノイズになりやすいです。一方 `:focus-visible` はキーボードナビゲーション時のみにアウトラインを表示するため、アクセシビリティと UX のバランスが取れています。 ```suggestion #messageInput:focus-visible { border-color: var(--vscode-focusBorder); } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/webview/chatAssets.ts Line: 31 Comment: **`:focus-visible` から `:focus` への変更によるアクセシビリティの後退** 旧 CSS では `#messageInput:focus-visible` を使用していましたが、新 CSS では `#messageInput:focus` に変わっています。`:focus` はマウスクリック時にもアウトラインを表示するため、視覚的にノイズになりやすいです。一方 `:focus-visible` はキーボードナビゲーション時のみにアウトラインを表示するため、アクセシビリティと UX のバランスが取れています。 ```suggestion #messageInput:focus-visible { border-color: var(--vscode-focusBorder); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `:focus-visible` pseudo-class was removed from this rule, which is an accessibility regression. Without it, the copy button won't become visible when a user navigates to it using the keyboard (e.g., with the Tab key). Please add it back to ensure the button is accessible to all users. To prevent regressions, add unit tests to verify the presence of critical accessibility attributes like `aria-label` in webviews.

```suggestion
.code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; }
```

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. To prevent regressions, add unit tests to verify the presence of critical accessibility attributes like `aria-label` in webviews.
</details>

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**静的解析の警告: if文の後にブレースが必要です。**

CIパイプラインのリンターが `Expected { after 'if' condition` の警告を出しています（Line 123）。



<details>
<summary>🔧 修正案</summary>

```diff
 export function isGeneratingSessionState(
   rawState: string | undefined,
 ): boolean {
-  if (!rawState) return false;
+  if (!rawState) {
+    return false;
+  }
   return GENERATING_SESSION_STATES.has(rawState);
 }
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
export function isGeneratingSessionState(
  rawState: string | undefined,
): boolean {
  if (!rawState) {
    return false;
  }
  return GENERATING_SESSION_STATES.has(rawState);
}
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

**このモジュール専用のテストファイルを作成してください。**

実装は適切で、`ChatMessageItem`インターフェース（`m.role`、`m.html`、`m.createTime`）と正しく整合しています。VSCode API のフォールバック処理やコピー機能のエラーハンドリングも適切です。

ただし、`src/**/*.ts`のコーディングガイドラインに従い、`src/webview/chatAssets.ts`モジュール専用のテストファイルが必要です。現在、`getChatWebviewHtml`を経由した間接的なテストのみ存在しますが、抽出されたこのモジュール自体の直接的なテストカバレッジが必須です。

**コピーボタンのキーボードフォーカス時の視認性が失われた（アクセシビリティ後退）** 旧 CSS では以下のようなセレクターが存在していました： ```css .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ``` 新しい CSS では `:focus-visible` の部分が削除されており、`.code-block:hover .copy-code-button` のみになっています。これにより、キーボードでタブ移動してコピーボタンにフォーカスした際にボタンが表示されなくなり、キーボード操作のアクセシビリティが低下します。 ```suggestion .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/webview/chatAssets.ts Line: 19 Comment: **コピーボタンのキーボードフォーカス時の視認性が失われた（アクセシビリティ後退）** 旧 CSS では以下のようなセレクターが存在していました： ```css .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ``` 新しい CSS では `:focus-visible` の部分が削除されており、`.code-block:hover .copy-code-button` のみになっています。これにより、キーボードでタブ移動してコピーボタンにフォーカスした際にボタンが表示されなくなり、キーボード操作のアクセシビリティが低下します。 ```suggestion .code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**`:focus-visible` から `:focus` への変更によるアクセシビリティの後退** 旧 CSS では `#messageInput:focus-visible` を使用していましたが、新 CSS では `#messageInput:focus` に変わっています。`:focus` はマウスクリック時にもアウトラインを表示するため、視覚的にノイズになりやすいです。一方 `:focus-visible` はキーボードナビゲーション時のみにアウトラインを表示するため、アクセシビリティと UX のバランスが取れています。 ```suggestion #messageInput:focus-visible { border-color: var(--vscode-focusBorder); } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/webview/chatAssets.ts Line: 31 Comment: **`:focus-visible` から `:focus` への変更によるアクセシビリティの後退** 旧 CSS では `#messageInput:focus-visible` を使用していましたが、新 CSS では `#messageInput:focus` に変わっています。`:focus` はマウスクリック時にもアウトラインを表示するため、視覚的にノイズになりやすいです。一方 `:focus-visible` はキーボードナビゲーション時のみにアウトラインを表示するため、アクセシビリティと UX のバランスが取れています。 ```suggestion #messageInput:focus-visible { border-color: var(--vscode-focusBorder); } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `:focus-visible` pseudo-class was removed from this rule, which is an accessibility regression. Without it, the copy button won't become visible when a user navigates to it using the keyboard (e.g., with the Tab key). Please add it back to ensure the button is accessible to all users. To prevent regressions, add unit tests to verify the presence of critical accessibility attributes like `aria-label` in webviews.

```suggestion
.code-block:hover .copy-code-button, .copy-code-button:focus-visible { opacity: 1; }
```

```
script behavior: mount a DOM with elements used by the script (chat, typing,
messageInput, sendButton, sessionLabel, composer) then execute the CHAT_JS
script in a jsdom environment and assert renderMessages/updateUI outcomes by
posting a "chatState" message (check that messages with properties matching
ChatMessageItem render correctly and timestamps are formatted), simulate
input/submit and Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to ensure vscode.postMessage is called with
{type:"sendMessage"}, and test the copy-button click flow (mock
navigator.clipboard.writeText to succeed/fail and assert button text changes).
Ensure tests also cover the fallback acquireVsCodeApi case by providing a fake
acquireVsCodeApi absence and spying the console fallback postMessage, and name
the file e.g. src/webview/chatAssets.test.ts to satisfy module-level coverage.
```

📝 Walkthrough ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## リリースノート * **リファクタリング** * チャットウィンドウの内部実装を整理し、モジュール構造を最適化しました。スタイルとスクリプト処理の管理方式を改善することで、システムの保守性と今後の拡張性が向上します。ユーザーが体験する機能と動作に変更はありません。 ## Walkthrough Webview の CSS と JavaScript アセットを `src/chatView.ts` から新しいモジュール `src/webview/chatAssets.ts` に分離しました。既存の CSS/JS の動作は変わらず、単にテンプレート定数としてインポートされるようになりました。また、複数の関数のコード整形が行われました。 ## Changes |コホート / ファイル|概要| |---|---| |**Webview アセット分離**
`src/webview/chatAssets.ts`|新規ファイル。`CHAT_CSS` と `CHAT_JS` をテンプレート定数としてエクスポート。chat UI のスタイリング、メッセージ表示、コピーボタン、型指示アニメーション、Webview API 初期化、メッセージハンドリングを含む。| |**チャットビュー統合**
`src/chatView.ts`|`CHAT_CSS` と `CHAT_JS` のインポートを追加し、`getChatWebviewHtml` でアセット定数を使用するように更新。`buildChatMessagesFromActivities`、`JulesChatViewProvider` メソッド、`getNonce` 関数のコード整形。関数署名は変更なし（フォーマットのみ）。| ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 2 (Simple) | ⏱️ ~10 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - **PR `#370`**: Chat UI のテキスト（コンポーザープレースホルダー、セッションラベル、送信ボタン）を修正しており、同じ chat UI 領域の変更であるため関連性がある。 ## Poem > 🐰 *CSS と JS を分けて整理して、* > *新しいファイルへ引っ越させて、* > *Chat view はすっきり、きれいになり、* > *レビューも楽に、保守も嬉しい！* > ✨

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues

issue_comments.body:
## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request addresses issue #419 by refactoring the chat webview assets. It moves the CSS and JavaScript code from the `chatView.ts` file into a dedicated `chatAssets.ts` file. This change enhances code organization and maintainability by separating concerns and improving readability. ### Highlights * **Refactoring**: This PR refactors the chat webview code by extracting the CSS and JavaScript from the `chatView.ts` file into a new file `chatAssets.ts`. * **Modularity**: By moving the CSS and JavaScript into a separate module, the code becomes more modular and easier to maintain. * **Readability**: The change improves the readability of `chatView.ts` by removing large string literals.
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

performed_via_github_app.name: Gemini Code Assist
performed_via_github_app.description:
**Accelerate code reviews and improve code quality**

Bring the power of [Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/) directly to GitHub, providing AI-powered code reviews on your pull requests, with automatic pull request summaries, ready-to-commit code suggestions and the ability to invoke Gemini for assistance at any point on the PR.

reviews.body:
### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `a98463b68e`
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](http://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the chat webview's CSS and JavaScript assets out of minified string literals in `chatView.ts` and into a new, more maintainable `src/webview/chatAssets.ts` file. This is a positive change for code organization and readability. My review identified two minor regressions in the new CSS file: an accessibility issue with the copy button's focus visibility and a visual bug with the typing indicator animation. The rest of the changes are primarily code formatting, which improves readability. I've also suggested adding unit tests to prevent accessibility regressions.

```
Inline comments:
In `@src/chatView.ts`:
- Around line 318-336: The handler registered in
webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage uses several single-line if statements
without braces (e.g., the check for message?.type === "requestInitialState",
message?.type !== "sendMessage", and the empty sessionId/text check), which
triggers static-analysis warnings; update those conditions in the
onDidReceiveMessage callback to always use block braces { ... } around their
bodies (so the call to this.postState(), the early return when type !==
"sendMessage", and the early return when !sessionId || !text are wrapped in
braces) to satisfy the linter and make the control flow explicit while leaving
the try { await this.onSendMessage(sessionId, text); } catch (...) block
unchanged.
- Around line 120-125: The linter warns about a missing block after the if
statement in the isGeneratingSessionState function; update the function so the
if (!rawState) branch uses braces (i.e., wrap the return false; in { ... }) to
satisfy the rule—modify the isGeneratingSessionState implementation that
references rawState and GENERATING_SESSION_STATES.has(rawState) accordingly.
- Around line 208-236: Add missing block braces for every single-line if in
chatView.ts so the parser stops warning; specifically wrap the bodies of if
(activity.sessionFailed?.reason), if (activity.planGenerated?.plan), if
((activity as any).gitPatch?.diff) and its inner if (typeof diff === "string" &&
diff.trim().length > 0), as well as if (activity.artifacts &&

inner if (diffData && typeof diffData === "string") with { ... } blocks; update

disappear.

In `@src/webview/chatAssets.ts`:

imports CHAT_JS and verifies the webview script behavior: mount a DOM with
```
